# NimLab — Game Theory & Reinforcement Learning Playground

## Summary
NimLab is an interactive educational web application that demonstrates how two fundamentally different approaches — mathematical optimization (XOR/Nim Sum strategy) and reinforcement learning (Q-Learning) — solve the same strategic decision-making problem: the Misère variant of the Nim game.

## Purpose
This project explores the boundary between algorithmic intelligence and learned intelligence. The XOR strategy computes the optimal move analytically, while Q-Learning discovers the same policy through experience. The comparison reveals when analytical solutions outperform learning, and when learning is the only option.

## Features
- Play against 4 AI strategies: Random, Greedy, Optimal (XOR), Q-Learning
- Explainable AI: Algorithm Inspector shows Nim Sum, binary representations, optimal moves, and reasoning
- Learning Mode: Interactive tutorials on XOR, Nim Sum, winning/losing positions, Misère endgame
- Algorithm Comparison: Simulate AI vs AI matches (up to 5000 games) and compare win rates
- Custom board configurations
- Move history and statistics tracking
- Two-player mode

## Technical Implementation
- Q-Learning with epsilon-greedy exploration, trained against a mixed-strategy opponent
- Correct Misère Nim strategy with endgame switching
- Pure JavaScript, jQuery, HTML/CSS — no build tools required
- localStorage for Q-values, statistics, and preferences

## Key Concepts Demonstrated
- Reinforcement Learning (Q-Learning, temporal difference, exploration vs exploitation)
- Combinatorial Game Theory (Nim Sum, Sprague-Grundy theorem foundations)
- Algorithm Design & Comparison
- Explainable AI / Transparent Decision-Making

## Author
Kruthik Koundinyas

Portfolio: https://kruthik.vercel.app

## Pages
- / (index.html) — Landing page with project overview
- /one-player.html — Play vs AI with Algorithm Inspector
- /learn.html — Interactive game theory and RL tutorials
- /compare.html — AI vs AI simulation and performance comparison
- /two-player.html — Two-player mode
